agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "SHIP WITH FIXES",
    "one_liner": "Off-by-one pagination bug skips first page of results; missing input validation allows SQL injection via negative LIMIT/OFFSET"
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Off-by-one error: page 1 skips the first pageSize rows",
      "risk": "Users requesting page=1 (the documented first page) get `OFFSET = 1 * pageSize`, skipping the entire first page of results. Page=0 returns the real first page, contradicting the API contract.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "getOrderHistory, line: `const offset = page * pageSize`",
      "reproduction": "Call `getOrderHistory('cust_1', 1, 20)`. Offset = 20, so the 20 most recent orders are silently skipped. The customer sees orders 21-40 as their 'first' page.",
      "fix": "Change to `const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize;`",
      "time_to_fix": "30 seconds",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "API documentation states pages are 1-indexed — current implementation is 0-indexed"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation allows negative offset, zero/negative pageSize, and SQL injection of unbounded queries",
      "risk": "No bounds checking on `page` or `pageSize`. A caller passing `page=0` or `page=-1` produces negative offsets (Postgres errors). `pageSize=0` returns nothing; `pageSize=-1` in some drivers bypasses LIMIT entirely, returning the full table. `pageSize=999999` causes a full table scan and potential OOM.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "getOrderHistory, function parameters — no validation before query execution",
      "reproduction": "Call `getOrderHistory('cust_1', -1, 50)` → Postgres error on negative offset. Call `getOrderHistory('cust_1', 1, 1000000)` → returns entire order history, potential denial of service.",
      "fix": "Add validation: `if (page < 1) throw new Error('page must be >= 1'); if (pageSize < 1 || pageSize > 100) throw new Error('pageSize must be 1-100');`",
      "time_to_fix": "2 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "API stability and resilience — unvalidated inputs can crash the query or dump excessive data"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * exposes all columns and OFFSET pagination degrades at scale",
      "risk": "`SELECT *` returns every column including potentially sensitive internal fields (soft-delete flags, internal notes, cost prices). Additionally, OFFSET-based pagination degrades linearly — `OFFSET 100000` forces Postgres to scan and discard 100K rows before returning results. For customers with large order histories, deep pages will time out.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "getOrderHistory, SQL query",
      "reproduction": "Call `getOrderHistory('cust_1', 5000, 20)` with offset=99980. Postgres scans ~100K rows to return 20. Response time degrades from ~5ms to seconds.",
      "fix": "Short-term: replace `SELECT *` with explicit column list. Long-term: switch to cursor/keyset pagination using `WHERE created_at < $lastSeenTimestamp ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $pageSize`.",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes for column list; 1 hour for keyset pagination",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Performance SLAs for customers with large order histories"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No total count returned — clients cannot render pagination controls",
      "description": "The function returns only `Order[]` with no metadata. Clients need at minimum `totalCount` or `hasNextPage` to render pagination UI. Without it, clients must fetch page N+1 to discover there are no more results.",
      "location": "getOrderHistory return type `Promise<Order[]>`"
    },
    {
      "title": "No index guarantee on (customer_id, created_at)",
      "description": "The query filters on `customer_id` and orders by `created_at DESC`. Without a composite index `(customer_id, created_at DESC)`, Postgres will do a sequential scan per customer query. This is not visible in the code but is a deployment prerequisite.",
      "location": "SQL WHERE/ORDER BY clause"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Return a typed response object `{ data: Order[], totalCount: number, page: number, pageSize: number, hasNextPage: boolean }` instead of a raw array",
    "Add a `customerId` format/existence check before hitting the database to fail fast on invalid IDs",
    "Consider adding a request-level timeout on the db.query call to prevent long-running queries from holding connections"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "The off-by-one bug means any existing consumers using page=1 are already seeing wrong data. Fixing the offset changes which rows every page returns — clients with cached page tokens or stored page numbers will see shifted results during rollout.",
    "rollback_capability": "Safe to roll back since this is a stateless query change. No migrations involved.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fixing the offset is a breaking change for any client currently compensating by using page=0. Coordinate with API consumers or version the endpoint.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring visible — add query duration logging and alerts for pageSize abuse or deep-offset queries.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": true,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["Fixing the off-by-one changes every page's result set — existing consumers will see shifted data"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert the offset formula change. No database or state changes involved."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": ["Off-by-one bug is definitively provable from the code and API contract", "Input validation gap is clear from the function signature", "OFFSET pagination scaling is well-documented PostgreSQL behavior"],
    "low_confidence_areas": ["Cannot confirm presence or absence of a composite index without seeing the schema/migrations", "Cannot confirm whether SELECT * exposes sensitive columns without seeing the table definition"],
    "assumptions": ["Database is PostgreSQL based on $1/$2/$3 parameterized query syntax", "No middleware layer validates page/pageSize before this function is called", "The API documentation accurately states 1-indexed pagination"]
  }
}
